title: Convert hub from WebView to native RecyclerView
description:
Replace the WebView-based hub (index.html/script.js/style.css) with a native Kotlin Activity using RecyclerView. This is a prerequisite for all mini-apps since they need a native launcher.

Sub-tasks:
- Replace MainActivity WebView with a RecyclerView listing mini-apps
- Read app registry from a Kotlin data structure (no more apps-metadata.json in WebView)
- Each app entry shows name and description, tapping launches the Activity
- Keep the info page accessible (convert to native or keep as a secondary WebView)
- Remove webkit dependency
- Dark theme styling matching current design (#121212 background, light text)
- Remove allowFileAccessFromFileURLs (no longer needed without WebView hub)

This replaces the JS bridge approach (PARANOID-1t9, closed).
close_reason: Implemented: replaced WebView hub with native RecyclerView, AppRegistry, InfoActivity. Removed webkit dep. Committed as 3dd6df2.

title: NetMap: hub integration (JS bridge for native launch)
description:
Add JavaScriptInterface to MainActivity so the WebView hub can launch native NetMap activities.

Sub-tasks:
- 7.1 Add JavaScriptInterface (ParanoidBridge) to MainActivity with launchApp(name) method
- 7.2 Add native: true field to apps-metadata.json for apps with registered Activities
- 7.3 Update build-metadata.sh to detect native apps and set the native flag
- 7.4 Update script.js to check app.native and call window.ParanoidBridge.launchApp(app.name)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/specs/netmap-hub-launch/spec.md
No dependencies on groups 2-6. Can run in parallel with groups 1-2.
close_reason: Hub should be native, not WebView. JS bridge approach dropped.

title: NetMap: foreground recording service
description:
Create the RecordingService foreground service that combines location + telephony into measurements.

Sub-tasks:
- 4.1 Create notification channel setup
- 4.2 Create RecordingService: foreground service (START_STICKY) combining location + telephony
- 4.3 Implement measurement buffering and batch DB writes (20 items or 30s)
- 4.4 Implement notification updates (duration, count, signal level every ~5s)
- 4.5 Implement onDestroy flush: save buffered measurements and mark recording ended on service kill
- 4.6 Implement service binding so Activity receives live measurement Flow

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/specs/netmap-recording/spec.md (Foreground Recording Service requirement)
Depends on: data layer (group 2), data sources (group 3)
close_reason: RecordingService implemented: foreground service with buffering, notification, binding, onDestroy flush. Builds clean. Committed as 4630ce6.

title: NetMap: map UI (NetMapActivity, RecordingsActivity, DetailActivity)
description:
Create all NetMap UI screens with MapLibre map, signal HUD, recording controls, and navigation.

Sub-tasks:
- 5.1 Create NetMapActivity layout (XML): MapLibre MapView, signal HUD, Start/Stop button, toolbar
- 5.2 Implement NetMapActivity: map init with Carto Dark Matter tiles, location centering
- 5.3 Implement runtime permission flow: request in order, rationale dialogs, Open Settings for permanently denied
- 5.4 Wire Start/Stop buttons to RecordingService (bind/unbind lifecycle)
- 5.5 Implement signal-colored polyline rendering on the map
- 5.6 Implement signal HUD (RSRP, network type, carrier, No signal fallback)
- 5.7 Implement toolbar navigation: overflow menu with Recordings item
- 5.8 Create RecordingsActivity (RecyclerView list, long-press delete, empty state)
- 5.9 Create RecordingDetailActivity (map + stats overlay, toolbar with back + export)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/specs/netmap-map-ui/spec.md
Depends on: data layer (group 2), data sources (group 3), recording service (group 4)
close_reason: All 3 activities + MapHelper implemented with layouts. Signal HUD, colored track, recordings list, detail view, permissions flow, service binding. Builds and tests pass. Committed as a88f5ae.

title: NetMap: data layer (Room DB, domain models, DAOs)
description:
Create the netmap-data capability: domain models, Room entities, DAOs, and database.

Sub-tasks:
- 2.1 Create domain models: Recording, Measurement, CellMeasurement, GeoPoint, enums
- 2.2 Create signal level computation function + unit tests
- 2.3 Create Room entities: RecordingEntity, MeasurementEntity (with cellsJson TypeConverter)
- 2.4 Create DAOs: RecordingDao (CRUD + empty-recording cleanup), MeasurementDao
- 2.5 Create ParanoidDatabase (Room database class)
- 2.6 Write instrumentation tests for DAOs (in-memory database)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/specs/netmap-data/spec.md
Depends on: project setup (group 1)
close_reason: Domain models, signal level calculator with tests, Room entities, DAOs, CellsJsonConverter, ParanoidDatabase all implemented. 14 unit tests pass. Committed as ab6fe60.

title: NetMap: data sources (LocationSource + TelephonySource)
description:
Create the data sources for GPS and cell info collection.

Sub-tasks:
- 3.1 Create LocationSource: Flow wrapper around FusedLocationProviderClient (configurable interval/distance, defaults 2s/5m)
- 3.2 Create TelephonySource: snapshot function mapping CellInfo subclasses to CellMeasurement + unit tests
- 3.3 Handle null/empty allCellInfo gracefully (empty cells list, no crash)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/specs/netmap-recording/spec.md (Location/Telephony Data Source requirements)
Depends on: project setup (group 1), data layer (group 2)
close_reason: LocationSource and TelephonySource implemented with tests. 9 unit tests pass. Committed as 006a41b.

title: NetMap: project setup and Gradle dependencies
description:
Add KSP plugin, Room, MapLibre, Play Services Location, Coroutines, kotlinx.serialization to build.gradle.kts. Add permissions and register activities/service in AndroidManifest.xml. Delete outdated netmap-android-spec.md. Verify project compiles.

Sub-tasks:
- 1.1 Add KSP plugin to root and app build.gradle.kts
- 1.2 Add dependencies: Room, MapLibre, Play Services Location, Coroutines, kotlinx.serialization
- 1.3 Add permissions to AndroidManifest.xml

- 1.5 Verify project compiles
- 1.6 Delete netmap-android-spec.md

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netmap-app/tasks.md group 1
No dependencies. Can run in parallel with group 7.
close_reason: All sub-tasks done: KSP, Room, MapLibre, Play Services Location, Coroutines, kotlinx.serialization added. Manifest permissions and activities registered. Stubs compile. Committed as bdecc9a.

title: UsageAudit: Build History and Export screen (5C)
description:
Why this issue exists: The History/Export screen depends on both the data layer (3B/3C) and the export module (a45). Building it as a separate ticket unblocks the Today and Last Night screens (5A/5B/5D) from waiting on a45.

What needs to be done:

**5C – History/Export screen**
- Red: failing test for History/Export screen recent locally available overnight reports list
- Green: smallest reports list implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for export action wiring (CSV and plain-text share flows)
- Green: smallest export action wiring to pass
- Refactor: simplify navigation and action wiring

Acceptance criteria:
- History/Export screen lists recent locally available overnight reports
- Export and share flows are wired to the export module (a45)
- UI remains Views-based, minimal, and offline-first
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Build Today and Last Night screens (5A/5B/5D)
description:
Why this issue exists: Today and Last Night are the primary user-facing screens. They depend only on the data layer (3B/3C) — not on export (a45) — so they can be built and verified as soon as data collection is done, without waiting for the History/Export screen.

What needs to be done:

**5A – Today screen**
- Red: failing test for Today screen empty state (no data available)
- Green: smallest empty state implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for Today screen populated summary and top apps list
- Green: smallest populated summary implementation to pass
- Refactor: simplify Today screen layout and binding logic

**5B – Last Night screen**
- Red: failing test for Last Night screen summary content
- Green: smallest summary content implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for incomplete-data warning, no-observed-activity wording, and attribution wording
- Green: smallest warning/wording implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for nights that include charging transitions presentation
- Green: smallest charging-transition presentation to pass
- Refactor: simplify Last Night presentation and labels

**5D – Presentation polish**
- Red: failing test for attribution-limit copy visibility where required
- Green: smallest copy change to pass
- Green: smallest copy/layout changes for remaining polish items
- Refactor: tidy layouts/resources for dark theme and OLED-friendly presentation (#121212 backgrounds, light text)

Wording rules to enforce in UI:
- 'observed activity' for app usage in a window
- 'suspected contributors' only when correlating with battery loss
- Never claim exact per-app battery percentages
- When no foreground activity: explicitly say no foreground app activity was observed
- When charging inside the window: explicitly note that the window included charging activity

Acceptance criteria:
- Today screen shows a useful empty state when no data is available
- Last Night screen clearly distinguishes observed activity from inferred drain contribution
- UI remains Views-based, minimal, and offline-first
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Add Android data layer — usage queries and battery collection (3B/3C)
description:
Why this issue exists: With permission gating in place (4tb), this ticket wires the Android data sources — UsageStatsManager queries and battery snapshot collection — into clean, testable adapters. These adapters feed the domain model (bmm) and unblock all UI work.

What needs to be done:

**3B – Usage queries**
- Red: failing test for mapping normalized Android usage results into AppUsageSlice
- Green: smallest usage-mapping adapter to pass
- Red: failing test for UsageStatsManager adapter for Today summaries
- Green: smallest adapter implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for overnight usage query adapter for configured overnight window
- Green: smallest overnight adapter to pass
- Red: failing test for package normalization and filtering (missing labels, noisy/system packages)
- Green: smallest normalization logic to pass
- Refactor: tidy Android usage-query mapping and boundaries

**3C – Battery snapshot collection**
- Red: failing test for battery-signal mapper converting Android battery signals into BatterySnapshot values
- Green: smallest mapper implementation to pass
- Red: failing integration test for persisting mapped BatterySnapshot through the collector seam
- Green: smallest collector-to-persistence wiring to pass
- Red: failing test for app-open snapshot capture (application lifecycle hook)
- Green: smallest app-open capture to pass
- Red: failing test for power-state-change snapshot capture (BroadcastReceiver)
- Green: smallest power-state-change receiver to pass
- Red: failing test for boot-receiver snapshot capture (BOOT_COMPLETED)
- Green: smallest BOOT_COMPLETED receiver to pass
- Refactor: reduce coupling between mappers, collectors, persistence, and app lifecycle hooks
- Manual: verify overnight snapshot behavior on a real device

Acceptance criteria:
- App can persist battery snapshots without network access
- App can query app activity for the configured overnight window
- Boot, power-state-change, and app-open triggers all capture battery snapshots
- Automatable tests cover data mapping and persistence seams; device-only flows are called out for manual verification
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Build minimal mini-app UI
description:
Why this issue exists: The three-screen Views-based UI is the user-facing layer that brings together data collection and export. It must be minimal, offline-first, and honest about what the data shows.

What needs to be done:

**5A – Today screen**
- Red/Green: Today screen empty state (no data available)
- Red/Green: Today screen populated summary and top apps list
- Refactor: simplify Today screen layout and binding logic

**5B – Last Night screen**
- Red/Green: Last Night screen summary content
- Red/Green: incomplete-data warning, no-observed-activity wording, and attribution wording
- Red/Green: nights that include charging transitions presentation
- Refactor: simplify Last Night presentation and labels

**5C – History/Export screen**
- Red/Green: History/Export screen actions (recent locally available overnight reports, export actions)
- Refactor: simplify navigation and action wiring

**5D – Presentation polish**
- Red/Green: attribution-limit copy visibility where required
- Green: smallest copy/layout changes
- Refactor: tidy layouts/resources for dark theme and OLED-friendly presentation (#121212 backgrounds, light text)

Wording rules to enforce in UI:
- 'observed activity' for app usage in a window
- 'suspected contributors' only when correlating with battery loss
- Never claim exact per-app battery percentages
- When no foreground activity: explicitly say no foreground app activity was observed
- When charging inside the window: explicitly note that the window included charging activity

Acceptance criteria:
- Today screen shows a useful empty state when no data is available
- Last Night screen clearly distinguishes observed activity from inferred drain contribution
- UI remains Views-based, minimal, and offline-first
close_reason: Replaced by PARANOID-c30 (Today/Last Night screens, 5A/5B/5D) and PARANOID-7fq (History/Export screen, 5C) to eliminate artificial serialization behind a45

title: UsageAudit: Validate on device
description:
Why this issue exists: Final validation pass to confirm unit tests, instrumentation tests, and manual device checks all pass and the app honestly presents battery information.

What needs to be done:
- Run unit tests for aggregators, summaries, exporters, and time-window edge cases
- Run instrumentation/UI tests for in-app permission gating, empty states, and screen flows
- Manually verify daytime usage summaries on a device with known app activity
- Manually verify the Settings handoff for usage access on a device
- Manually verify overnight battery audit behavior across at least one real night window
- Manually verify wording for nights with no observed app activity and for nights with charging transitions
- Verify CSV and plain-text share flows with at least one external target app
- Perform a wording audit: user-facing battery text must never claim exact per-app drain
- Tidy documentation and mark all completed tasks as done in openspec/changes/add-usage-audit-app/tasks.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Test runs are green
- Manual checks confirm the app reports battery loss honestly without claiming exact per-app drain
- Export/share flows produce usable outputs for real-world sharing

title: UsageAudit: Integrate mini-app into paranoid hub
description:
Why this issue exists: The new UsageAudit mini-app must be registered in AndroidManifest, wired into the hub's navigation, and have its functionality spec updated to match the shipped behavior.

What needs to be done:
- Red: failing integration/UI check for direct in-app navigation entry launching the mini-app from the hub
- Green: add the new mini-app activity, resources, and manifest/navigation wiring
- Red: identify any missing export/file-provider wiring through a failing integration check
- Green: add only the required export/file-provider wiring
- Refactor: tidy manifest/resource/navigation duplication
- Add or update usageaudit/spec/functionality.md to match the implemented behavior

Acceptance criteria:
- Mini-app is launchable from the main app via one direct navigation entry
- Implementation-facing mini-app spec matches the shipped behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Implement export and sharing
description:
Why this issue exists: Users need to get audit results out of the app — as a compact human-readable summary for sharing and as a structured CSV for later analysis. Both formats must work fully offline.

What needs to be done:

**4A – Text summaries**
- Red/Green: Today plain-text summary formatter
- Red/Green: Last Night plain-text summary formatter
- Refactor: unify summary formatting patterns and wording

Summary content: report type, report window, total usage or battery start/end/delta, top apps with foreground duration, warning lines for incomplete battery coverage, charging transitions, or no observed app activity.

**4B – CSV export**
- Red/Green: CSV rows/headers for v1 schema:
  - report_date, window_start, window_end, app_label, package_name, foreground_duration_millis, battery_start_percent, battery_end_percent, battery_delta_percent, warning_flags
  - warning_flags: delimited field with values like incomplete_battery_data, charging_transition, no_observed_app_activity
- Refactor: simplify CSV generation and escaping rules

**4C – Android sharing**
- Red/Green: instrumentation/integration test for launching text/file sharing
- Green: smallest FileProvider/sharesheet wiring to pass
- Refactor: tidy export file lifecycle and share intent creation

Wording rules:
- 'observed activity' for app usage found inside a report window
- 'suspected contributors' only when correlating app activity with battery loss
- Never claim exact per-app battery percentages

Acceptance criteria:
- User can share a compact human-readable summary from the visible report
- User can export a CSV file and send it through Android sharing
- Export works offline using only local data
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Add usage access permission gating (3A)
description:
Why this issue exists: Usage access permission gating is a prerequisite for all data collection. The app needs a clean permission-check abstraction and clear UX for screens that require the usage access permission — before any data-layer wiring can be tested end-to-end.

What needs to be done:

**3A – Usage access gating**
- Red: failing test for permission-check abstraction for usage-access-gated screens
- Green: smallest permission-check implementation to pass
- Red: failing test for in-app empty state and Settings handoff UI when usage access is missing
- Green: smallest Settings handoff UI to pass
- Refactor: simplify permission-gating wiring and copy
- Manual: verify the Settings handoff works on a device

Acceptance criteria:
- App shows a clear action when usage access is missing
- Automatable tests cover the in-app gating seam; device-only flows are called out for manual verification
close_reason: Closed

title: UsageAudit: Build domain model and summary calculators
description:
Why this issue exists: The usage-audit mini-app needs pure, testable domain logic for daily phone usage and overnight battery auditing before any Android wiring can be built. This is the foundation all other tickets depend on.

What needs to be done:

**2A – Daily usage aggregation**
- Red: failing unit test for total daily foreground usage time
- Green: smallest daily aggregation logic to pass
- Red: failing unit test for ranking apps by foreground duration
- Green: smallest ranking logic to pass
- Refactor: tidy naming, data shapes, and duplication in the daily usage aggregator

**2B – Overnight audit calculation**
- Red/Green pairs for: battery delta from snapshots, app activity correlation (observed foreground overlapping the window), incomplete-window warnings, empty-activity nights, charging transitions inside the window, cross-midnight and timezone-safe summaries
- Refactor: tidy overnight audit calculator and shared summary types

**2C – Persistence model**
- First step: add Room runtime, KSP/kapt, and Room testing dependencies to android/app/build.gradle.kts before writing any entity code
- Red/Green for persisting and reading BatterySnapshot records via Room entities/DAO
- Persist only what v1 actually needs for History/Export (no speculative storage)
- Refactor: simplify persistence boundaries

Domain model:
- BatterySnapshot: timestamp, batteryPercent, chargingState, batteryStatus, batteryHealth (if available)
- AppUsageSlice: packageName, appLabel, windowStart, windowEnd, foregroundDurationMillis, launchCountEstimate (optional)
- OvernightAudit: startTime, endTime, battery start/end/delta, totalForegroundMillis, activeAppsCount, hadChargingTransition, hasIncompleteBatteryCoverage, notes/warnings

Tests operate on normalized AppUsageSlice values, not raw Android framework objects.

Acceptance criteria:
- Daily summaries produce total usage time and ranked apps by foreground duration
- Overnight summaries produce start/end timestamps, battery start/end/delta
- Overnight summaries return incomplete-data warnings when snapshots don't fully cover the window
- Persisted storage limited to what v1 needs for local audit history and export
notes: Run all tests via: just test (Docker required — host Java is incompatible with the Android toolchain). Target package: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/usageaudit/
close_reason: Closed

title: NetDiag: Polish
description:
Final polish: empty states, error handling, and build verification.

Tasks:
- 7.1 Empty states: no sessions, no signal, capture failed
- 7.2 Error handling: timeout during capture, Bluetooth failure, invalid JSON import, transport change mid-capture
- 7.3 Verify build and release workflow produces working APK with NetDiag

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/proposal.md
acceptance_criteria: APK builds and runs, empty states shown correctly, error handling covers timeout/Bluetooth failure/invalid JSON/transport change.
notes: Verification: ./gradlew connectedAndroidTest + manual test on device with airplane mode, BT off, malformed JSON import.
close_reason: Implemented: no-network empty state, 30s capture timeout, transport-change toast, file import with invalid-JSON handling. Build+tests pass.

title: NetDiag: UI
description:
Build the four NetDiag screens: capture wizard, snapshot detail, comparison results, session history.

Tasks:
- 6.1 Create NetDiagActivity layout (XML): permission prompts, capture button, progress indicators, receive-snapshot options, compare-with-saved option
- 6.2 Implement NetDiagActivity: permission flow, capture engine integration, exchange flow, device label assignment (auto from Build.MODEL, editable, "(A)"/"(B)" suffix for same model)
- 6.3 Create SnapshotDetailActivity layout + implementation: expandable sections for each data domain
- 6.4 Create ComparisonResultActivity layout + implementation: summary card, category breakdown, expandable findings with severity color coding
- 6.5 Create SessionHistoryActivity layout + implementation: RecyclerView list, long-press delete, empty state
- 6.6 Implement runtime permission request flow: runtime permissions (ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION/NEARBY_WIFI_DEVICES, READ_PHONE_STATE, POST_NOTIFICATIONS) sequential with rationale. Normal permissions (ACCESS_WIFI_STATE, ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE, INTERNET) are auto-granted. Bluetooth permissions on-demand before exchange, fallback to QR/file on denial.
- 6.7 Apply dark theme (#121212 background, light text) to all NetDiag screens
- 6.8 Implement severity color coding: red (CRITICAL), amber (WARNING), blue (INFO)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-ui/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/, android/app/src/main/res/layout/
acceptance_criteria: All 4 screens render correctly with dark theme, permission flow works, severity colors applied, device labels assigned correctly.
notes: Task 6.4: handle self-comparison case — show warning when both snapshots have the same ID (see netdiag-comparison spec). Task 6.2: add unit test for device label assignment logic (pure function — testable without devices; covers same-model '(A)'/'(B)' suffix).
close_reason: All tasks 6.1-6.8 already implemented: layouts, activities, permissions, dark theme, severity colors

title: NetDiag: Snapshot Exchange
description:
Implement Bluetooth, QR code, and file-based snapshot transfer between devices.

Tasks:
- 5.1 Implement Bluetooth RFCOMM exchange: server/client roles, snapshot JSON transfer, runtime permissions (API 31+), error handling for connection drops
- 5.2 Implement QR code encoding (chunked at ~1.5 KB/code with sequence headers, ~11 codes for 15 KB accounting for header overhead) and decoding (sequential scan with missing-chunk prompts)
- 5.3 Implement file export via FileProvider + share sheet (JSON)
- 5.4 Implement file import via share intent and file picker with malformed-JSON error handling and 1 MB size limit
- 5.5 Write unit tests for QR chunking/reassembly logic
- 5.6 Write instrumentation tests for Bluetooth and file exchange

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-exchange/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/
acceptance_criteria: Bluetooth transfer completes in <10s for 30 KB payload, QR chunking round-trips correctly, file import rejects malformed JSON and files >1 MB, all tests pass.

title: NetDiag: Comparison Engine
description:
Implement the pure-Kotlin comparison engine that diffs two snapshots.

Parallel track: can run concurrently with Exchange and UI once Data Layer is done.

Tasks:
- 4.1 Port ComparisonEngine.compare() from DiagnosticsSchema.kt (pure Kotlin, no Android deps)
- 4.2 Port all sub-comparisons: compareConnectivity, compareIpConfig, compareWifi, compareCellular, compareDns, compareProbes, compareThroughput, compareDeviceHealth. All sub-comparisons run regardless of overall status; INCOMPARABLE only affects final status derivation.
- 4.3 Add capture time delta warning (>5 minutes -> WARNING finding)
- 4.4 Port scoring (additive penalties: CRITICAL=0.4, WARNING=0.15, INFO=0.05) and status derivation logic. Self-comparison (same snapshot ID) returns IDENTICAL with no findings.
- 4.5 Write unit tests for each comparison function with known snapshot pairs
- 4.6 Write unit tests for edge cases: null fields, missing probes, incomparable transports, stale capture time delta, self-comparison

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-comparison/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/
acceptance_criteria: All comparison functions produce correct findings/scores, unit tests pass including edge cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: NetDiag: Snapshot Collectors
description:
Implement all network diagnostics collectors and the capture engine.

Tasks:
- 3.1 Create WifiCollector: WifiManager + WifiInfo (API 31+: via NetworkCapabilities.getTransportInfo()) -> WifiSnapshot, scan results -> WifiEnvironment, BSS Load IE parsing (API 30+), graceful degradation on API 26-29. Note: legacy getLinkSpeed() TX value available on all APIs; getTxLinkSpeedMbps()/getRxLinkSpeedMbps() are API 29+ only.
- 3.2 Create CellularCollector: TelephonyCallback (API 31+) / PhoneStateListener -> CellularSnapshot
- 3.3 Create DnsCollector: DnsResolver (API 29+) probes with rcode + latency, fallback to InetAddress for API 26-28, DoH cross-check for hijack detection
- 3.4 Create IpConfigCollector: LinkProperties -> IpConfig with anomaly flags
- 3.5 Create ProbeCollector: icmp4a ping to each ProbeTarget (5 packets, all targets concurrent), OkHttp EventListener HTTP timing to connectivitycheck.gstatic.com
- 3.6 Create DeviceHealthCollector: battery, Doze, Data Saver, airplane mode, VPN, memory, permissions
- 3.7 Create ConnDiagCollector: ConnectivityDiagnosticsManager data (API 30+), null on API 26-29
- 3.8 Create SnapshotCaptureEngine: orchestrate all collectors concurrently via coroutineScope { async {} }, assemble DiagnosticsSnapshot, detect transport changes during capture. Return error (not empty snapshot) when ALL collectors fail.
- 3.9 Write unit tests for each collector's data mapping logic (mock Android APIs where needed)
- 3.10 Write integration test for SnapshotCaptureEngine (instrumentation test on real device)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-snapshot/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/
acceptance_criteria: All collectors produce correctly-typed outputs, engine completes within 15s target, unit tests pass, total-failure returns error.
notes: Collectors 3.1-3.7 are independent and can be implemented in any order. 3.8 (engine) depends on all. Write unit tests (3.9) alongside each collector.

title: NetDiag: Data Layer

Create the data model and persistence layer for NetDiag.

- 2.2 Create Room entities: DiagnosticsSessionEntity, DiagnosticsSnapshotEntity (JSON TEXT column), DiagnosticsComparisonEntity (JSON TEXT column)
- 2.3 Create Room DAOs: SessionDao (CRUD, list by date desc), SnapshotDao, ComparisonDao
- 2.4 Register entities in ParanoidDatabase
- 2.5 Write unit tests for JSON serialization round-trip of DiagnosticsSnapshot
- 2.6 Write unit tests for enum ordering (severity, signal category)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-data/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/data/
acceptance_criteria: Data classes compile, Room entities registered, serialization round-trip tests pass, enum ordering tests pass.
close_reason: Room entities, DAOs, ParanoidDatabase registration (v2 migration), and 7 unit tests all done

title: NetMap: export (GeoJSON, CSV, KML, GPX + FileProvider)
description:
Implement all four export formats and the share mechanism.

Sub-tasks:
- 6.1 Implement GeoJSON exporter + unit test (golden file)
- 6.2 Implement CSV exporter + unit test (golden file)
- 6.3 Implement KML exporter + unit test (golden file)
- 6.4 Implement GPX exporter + unit test (golden file)
